stemHtml: How does the Instrument Landing System (ILS) guide an aircraft during approach and landing?

options.a: The aircraft's ILS receiver senses the glide slope and localiser signals transmitted from the ground
options.b: The glide slope transmitter senses the aircraft's position and adjusts the signal accordingly
options.c: The ILS ground station tracks the aircraft and sends directional commands to the autopilot
options.d: The aircraft's onboard radar system calculates the glide slope and localiser paths independently
